seoTitle: Build a Gantt Chart in Confluence: 2026 Step-by-Step Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Gantt chart in Confluence using the 2026 Cloud database layout, map task dependencies, and publish a live project timeline for your team.
intro: If your project timelines live on disconnected spreadsheets, keeping stakeholders aligned gets messy fast. This guide walks through building a Gantt chart directly inside Confluence's 2026 database layout, so your team can see start dates, dependencies, and milestones in one shared, always-current view.

steps.heading: Open the timeline container
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type the `/gantt` or `/database` slash command to deploy the primary timeline block.

steps.heading: Add date columns
steps.body: Click Add Column and set the field type to Date to capture your start and end milestones for each task row.

steps.heading: Map task dependencies
steps.body: Drag the visual progress handles inside the chart view to connect related tasks and show processing order.

steps.heading: Highlight the critical path
steps.body: Open the right-hand options drawer and toggle on the Critical Path filter to surface the tasks that drive your deadline.

steps.heading: Publish and share
steps.body: Give the timeline a clear project title, click Publish, and grab the public URL to share the live, auto-syncing calendar with stakeholders outside your workspace.

faq.question: Do I need a marketplace add-on to build a Gantt chart in Confluence?
faq.answer: No. The 2026 Cloud database layout supports native Gantt-style timelines, though tools like Gantt Chart for Confluence or MacroRoad can add extra features if you need them.

faq.question: Will the chart update automatically if a deadline shifts?
faq.answer: Yes, the timeline bars are linked to the underlying date fields, so adjusting a due date updates the visual bar without rebuilding the chart.

faq.question: Can I share the chart with people outside my Confluence workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, publishing the page and grabbing its public URL lets you share a live, auto-syncing view with external stakeholders.

faq.question: Can I color-code milestones and releases?
faq.answer: Yes, the customization options let you set distinct tracking colors for milestones and team release boundaries.
